metrics.narrative: No quantified value case exists. The 26 Feb demo surfaced qualitative pain only: a buyer speaker described that 'stores report incidents via web-based task system that emails a predefined chain; replies outside system email are lost', and that 'incident and supplier performance tracked manually in Excel scorecards until system implementation'. These point to manual effort and lost incident data but no dollar, headcount, or cycle-time figure was ever stated. In a competitive RFP, a quantified cost-of-status-quo is what makes the response score on value, not just fit — build it with Josh before submission.

champion.narrative: No champion yet, but Leslie McElhaney is the developable candidate: she is the one contact who replies, and she proactively confirmed on 4 Jun that Zycus remains included in the S2P RFP — a small but real act of advocacy. She has not yet introduced Zycus to power or shared competitive intel. Converting Leslie from inclusion-confirmer into an active sponsor who brokers EB access and shares the evaluation criteria is the relationship priority before the RFP scores.

competition.narrative: The competitive field is undisclosed. The buyer is running a formal, competitive RFP via Symphony eSourcing but has not named the shortlist. Notably, Zycus already withdrew from the narrower incident RFP on 12 Mar 2026 because 'fitment gaps too high', signalling at least one rival fit that narrower scope better. For the broader S2P RFP, surfacing who else is shortlisted is essential to position differentiation; competition is a genuine knowledge gap, not a no-competitor situation.

identify_pain.narrative: Operational pain is articulated but not owned at the executive level. A buyer speaker described stores reporting incidents via a web task system where 'replies outside system email are lost', and supplier performance tracked manually in Excel scorecards. The pain owner appears to be the supply-chain/operations team (Josh Ullrich). The financial weight of this pain has not been established with a budget owner.

paper_process.narrative: Some paper mechanics are known: the prospect has signed an NDA, and procurement runs through Symphony eSourcing for RFP distribution. Beyond NDA and the RFP channel, contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal, InfoSec) have not been discussed. Visibility will improve once the RFP releases and a shortlist forms.

sources: SF account contacts (not yet opp stakeholders)
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is engaged. The 26 Feb demo was attended by operational-tier contacts (Leslie McElhaney, Josh Ullrich, Gina Rendar); a buyer speaker noted decisions route up ('when we reconvene, then we can get me in there'). Account-level finance and C-suite contacts exist in Salesforce (Michael Blair, Chief Financial & Strategy Officer; Chris Ward, Chief Supply Chain Officer; Minh Le, Chief Information Officer) but none has been proven involved in this opportunity. The path to power runs through Leslie, the one responsive contact — broker the introduction, do not cold-outreach the EB on a tender.

sources: SF Next_Step__c history (12 Mar, 2 Mar, 22 Jun 2026)
decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal RFP via the Symphony eSourcing system. Sequence: pre-RFP demo 26 Feb, a narrower Supplier Incident Management RFP received 2 Mar from which Zycus withdrew on 12 Mar citing fitment gaps, while staying engaged for the broader full S2P RFP. That S2P RFP release has slipped May→Q3→first week of July. People engaged are operational; escalation to budget authority has not occurred. The process is buyer-led and paused pending release.

decision_criteria.narrative: Partial criteria are visible from the demo. The buyer prioritised supplier governance and incident management ('Evaluate Zycus iSupplier and iRisk modules for supplier governance and incident management as top priority') and probed configurability of incident stages and KPI scorecards. A full Source-to-Pay overview covering direct and indirect spend is a stated next-phase criterion. The formal criteria will be set by the RFP itself, which has not yet been released — so the live play is to influence the evaluation framework before it locks, while Zycus still has a relationship.

flags: Influence the spec before it locks. Zycus is confirmed included (Leslie, 4 Jun) but has no line into the evaluation criteria. The move is to use the still-open full-suite demo commitment as a value reason to re-contact Leslie, and ask what the S2P RFP will weight and who else is shortlisted — shaping the criteria around iSupplier governance and Merlin AI before the spec is frozen.

Broker EB access through the champion. All engaged contacts are operational; CSCO Chris Ward and CFO Michael Blair are likely budget owners but unengaged. On a tender, route the EB ask through Leslie ('who should we align the business case with on your side?'), not a cold approach.
No quantified business case: pain confirmed (SIM and S2P inefficiency) but no cost savings, cycle time, error rate, or ROI target captured. Build it with Josh so the RFP response scores on value.
Single-threaded operationally: only Leslie, Josh, Gina engaged. No multi-threading to EB, DM, or executive sponsor; add buyer contact roles to the opportunity.
Competitive blind spot in a formal RFP: Zycus already lost the narrower incident RFP on fitment — ask Leslie directly who else is shortlisted so the S2P response is tailored to win, not just respond.
Timeline reality: RFP overdue and 27 Aug close not credible from Qualified — set the forecast to Pipeline until the RFP releases and a real evaluation date exists.
best_practice_check.summary: This is a Qualified-stage RFP/tender deal whose forward motion depends on a twice-slipped release. The three highest-leverage levers, in tender order: (1) keep Leslie warm and use her to shape the evaluation criteria and disclose the shortlist before the spec locks; (2) broker EB access through Leslie, not cold; (3) build the quantified value case so the response scores on ROI, not just fit. Do NOT chase the buyer for a status update — silence during drafting is process, and pestering burns the one responsive contact.

competitive_position.summary: Competition is undisclosed. The buyer is running a formal Symphony eSourcing RFP, so rivals exist, but none has been named and Competitors__c is blank. Zycus's withdrawal from the narrower incident-management RFP on 12 Mar (fitment gaps) implies a competitor is positioned there. The only positive is the prospect calling Zycus a Gartner Magic Quadrant leader (Gina Rendar, 26 Feb). Surfacing the shortlist and the evaluation criteria is the top competitive gap — and the way to avoid a second fitment loss on the broader scope.

deltas.subject: Develop Leslie McElhaney as a champion to gain competitive intelligence and EB access. deltas.subject: Map and engage the Economic Buyer before RFP submission. Close date is 27 Aug 2026 (62 days out), but RFP has not been released (expected first week of July, now overdue). From Qualified stage, the deal must complete RFP submission, evaluation, shortlist, vendor selection, commercials, and contracting in under 9 weeks, which is not credible without immediate buyer re-engagement and an accelerated process.

Re-engage the buyer immediately to restart momentum. The deal has been quiet for 119 days (last buyer touch 27 Feb demo call). Rep has followed up six times since with no buyer reply. The RFP is now overdue (expected first week of July per 22 Jun note). Without buyer engagement in the next 7 days, the close date (27 Aug, 62 days out) is not credible. The move is to call or email Leslie, Josh, and Gina directly, referencing the 4 Jun confirmation that Zycus is included, and ask for an immediate update on RFP status and next steps.

Identify and secure a champion before the RFP evaluation begins. No internal advocate has emerged from the 26 Feb demo or subsequent outreach. Leslie, Josh, and Gina are operational contacts who have not introduced Zycus to decision-makers, shared competitive intel, or driven the process. The account-level contact list includes a Chief Supply Chain Officer (the likely senior pain owner for supplier governance and incident management) who has not been engaged. The move is to deliver immediate value to one of the current contacts (e.g., a supplier-incident-management best-practice guide, a peer QSR reference using Zycus for supplier scorecarding) and ask for an introduction to the CSCO or the RFP evaluation lead. Without a champion, win probability in a competitive RFP is low.

Position Merlin AI as the RFP differentiation wedge. The buyer expressed interest in 'AI-driven supplier onboarding and flexible KPI/scorecard builder' on the 26 Feb demo call. Zycus has Merlin Intake (AI-powered intake and onboarding) and I2O (AI negotiation intelligence), which are differentiators in an RFP scenario. The move is to tailor the RFP response (when it arrives) to highlight Merlin's AI capabilities for automating supplier onboarding and generating negotiation insights from supplier performance data, directly addressing the buyer's stated need. If the RFP questions allow, propose a Merlin-focused use-case demo or pilot as part of the evaluation process.
